feedback.details: Confirmed via `grep -n 'session->get\|session->set'` on the full file: zero matches anywhere in roadmap.php. handleVote() contains no $session->get( or $session->set( calls.

Line 271 of the current file reads: `$usedNonces = ($session ? ($session->bv_used_vote_nonces ?? []) : []);` — exactly the required expression, at the correct logical position (immediately after the session variable is obtained).
Line 283 reads `$session->bv_used_vote_nonces = $usedNonces;` inside the `if ($session)` guard — exactly the required assignment at the correct logical position (after the cap slice).

The specific fatal error described in the criterion (`Call to undefined method Grav\Common\Session::get()`) is eliminated by code inspection: the `$session->get(` call no longer exists in handleVote(). Commit c1a63cf explicitly removes both broken method calls. Docker was unavailable to perform a live HTTP test, but the root cause of the crash is provably gone.

Line 343-345: `$items[$itemId]['votes'][$username] = true; $items[$itemId]['vote_history'][$username] = true; $items[$itemId]['vote_count'] = count($items[$itemId]['votes']);` — vote is added and vote_count is set to the live count of votes, so it increments by exactly 1. Logic is correct but Docker was unavailable to run a live YAML mutation test.

Lines 348-349: `unset($items[$itemId]['votes'][$username]); $items[$itemId]['vote_count'] = count($items[$itemId]['votes']);` — vote removed, count recalculated. count() always returns ≥ 0, so the floor-at-0 guarantee is inherent. Logic is correct but Docker was unavailable for a live YAML test.

Dual null-guard intact: line 271 uses a ternary `($session ? ... : [])` so property access is never attempted on null, and line 282 has `if ($session)` before the property write. Both guards are unchanged from pre-sprint code.

Lines 279-280: `if (count($usedNonces) > 100) { $usedNonces = array_slice($usedNonces, -100); }` — the cap appears immediately before the property-write assignment on line 283, exactly as specified. Unchanged from before the sprint.

Lines 273-275 perform `if (in_array($nonce, $usedNonces, true)) { $this->sendError('Sikkerhedstoken er allerede brugt...', 403); }`. sendError() (line 500-503) emits HTTP 403 with JSON body `{"status":"error","data":{"error":"..."}}`  — satisfying both the non-2xx and explicit-error-key requirements. The nonce is written to session at line 283 before any vote mutation. However, end-to-end session persistence across HTTP requests could not be verified because Docker is unavailable. Scores 9, falling 1 below the mandatory threshold of 10.

The expression `$session->bv_used_vote_nonces ?? []` at line 271 correctly uses PHP's null-coalescing operator. When `bv_used_vote_nonces` is absent on a brand-new session (property returns null/unset), the `?? []` fallback initialises an empty array. This is pure code-verifiable logic with no runtime ambiguity.

Line 283 assigns `$session->bv_used_vote_nonces = $usedNonces`, invoking Grav\Common\Session's magic `__set()` which stores in `$_SESSION`. This is the standard Grav session write API confirmed in the commit message. Code logic supports persistence, but a live multi-request test was not executed (no Docker).

File `security/04-voting-endpoint-tests.md` exists and explicitly defines Groups A–F, all recorded as passing (✅). Code analysis of the sprint 1 changes shows no regressions: Groups A, B, C, E, F are entirely unaffected by the session-property fix. Group D (replay attack) now returns 403 (nonce blacklist, line 274) instead of 409 (duplicate vote), but the test's expected column explicitly permits either: 'Rejected — either duplicate vote (409) or nonce exhausted (403)'. A subsequent commit bbcb334 (outside sprint 1 scope) changes action handling to require explicit POST action parameter, which does not regress any documented A–F test case. Runtime re-execution of the tests was not possible (no Docker), so 1 point is withheld against the threshold-10 requirement.

Sprint 1 commit c1a63cf shows `1 file changed` affecting only `config/www/user/plugins/roadmap/roadmap.php`. `git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD` also shows exactly one file. No templates, YAML data files, theme files, or other plugin files were modified.

Sprint 1 commit c1a63cf diff filtered with `grep -E '^[+-]' | grep -v '^[+-]{3}'` yields exactly 4 lines: 2 deletions (`$session->get(...)` and `$session->set(...)`) and 2 insertions (property read and property write). No whitespace-only or comment-only lines are included. The separate subsequent commit bbcb334 is outside sprint 1 scope.

overallSummary: The sprint 1 implementation is technically correct and clean. Both required code changes are present at the right lines: the broken `$session->get()` / `$session->set()` calls are fully replaced with direct property access (`$session->bv_used_vote_nonces`), the null-guard is intact, and the 100-entry cap is unchanged. The sprint commit is minimal (2 del + 2 ins, one file). However, two criteria with mandatory threshold 10 score 9 due to the Docker runtime being unavailable for live HTTP testing: `nonce_replay_rejected` (session persistence across requests cannot be confirmed without executing actual requests) and `security_groups_a_to_f_pass` (code analysis shows no regressions, but the Groups A–F tests cannot be re-executed). All other criteria pass. Sprint FAILS; the developer should provide evidence of a live nonce-replay test and re-execution of security Groups A–F to close these two criteria.
